vacancySearchResults.jobTitle: Inclusion Advisor Psychologist - Register of Interest
vacancySearchResults.section: Student Wellbeing and Inclusion Programs and Services - Client Services
vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: Susi Meyers, Assistant Director Client Services on 0499 873 225 or susanne.meyers@education.nt.gov.au
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
This Register of Interest is for multiple Inclusion Advisor Psychologist job opportunities available throughout the Northern Territory. Applications may be assessed while the Register remains open or immediately on its closure in March 2025.

Psychologists work within a Multi-tiered System of Support framework to build capacity of educators to support students to maximise their educational outcomes. School psychologists perform psychological assessments with students for various reasons using psychometric testing, observation and interviews with parents and teachers. Consultation, comprehensive assessment, treatment planning and short term therapeutic interventions are provided to students with learning, behaviour and personal/social competence concerns that may be having a direct impact on educational outcomes.

Successful applicants must hold a degree in psychology and registration as a psychologist with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency. Copies of your qualifications and proof of professional membership status must be provided for your application to be considered. 

Selection will be made at the P1, P2 or P3 level depending on available positions and the experience/qualifications of successful applicants. Applicants may request to be based in various locations throughout the NT.
vacancySearchResults.specialInstructions:
Aboriginal applicants will be granted priority consideration for this vacancy.

Special Instructions: Aboriginal applicants will be granted priority consideration for this vacancy. Applicants must provide copies of their degree in psychology and proof of registration as psychologist with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Authority (AHPRA) to be considered. Work VISA documentation must be provided if you are not an Australian citizen.

vacancySearchResults.jobTitle: Nurse Practitioner Candidate / Nurse Practitioner - Primary Health
vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: Sarah Hore on 08 8955 6112 or sarah.hore@nt.gov.au
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective: The Nurse Practitioner is responsible for providing and leading advanced and extended clinical care within the primary health clinics in Central Australia Region. Nurses classified at this level are nurse leaders within primary health clinics and have a significant degree of autonomy and decision making within their scope of clinical practice. The nurse practitioners in primary health care support and enhance current service delivery as part of the multi-disciplinary team. The role provides primary health care services that focus on the first point of care; providing care across the lifespan; with focuses on health promotion and disease prevention; using population and public health strategies. Whilst a postgraduate qualification is an essential requirement for ongoing appointment to this vacancy, all applicants will be assessed. If the most suitable applicant does not hold the qualification, they may be appointed on a fixed basis with ongoing appointment subject to attaining the qualification during the fixed term. Several vacancies are available, either ongoing or fixed for 2 years. Several vacancies are available in various locations throughout the Northern Territory.
vacancySearchResults.specialInstructions: Aboriginal applicants will be granted priority consideration for this vacancy
